div.head: PM–47. Editorial Note
div.p: On May 8, 1960, presidential elections were held in Panama to select a successor to President Ernesto de la Guardia, Jr., whose four-year term expired in the autumn. Dr. Roberto F. Chiari was elected the new President of Panama, and he assumed office on October 1, 1960. Documentation concerning the election campaign is in Department of State file 719.00.
